steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The blank connects two sentences: the first says tickers were used in historic parades; the second says tickers had long since been retired by 2008. The transition must signal that the retirement was natural and expected—not a surprising contradiction and not a causal result of the parades.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'For example,': signals that the second sentence gives an instance of the first. But the 2008 detail introduces a contrast (no tickers), not an example of tickers being used. ✗
  • Choice B — 'In addition,': signals that the second sentence adds parallel information. The second sentence doesn't add another fact about tickers in use—it says they were gone. ✗
  • Choice C — 'Of course,': signals that what follows is expected or unsurprising. The phrase “long since retired” conveys exactly that the absence of tickers by 2008 was a natural, established reality—not a shock. ✓
  • Choice D — 'As a result,': signals the second sentence is a consequence of the first. But the tickers weren't retired because of the parades—this reverses the actual logic. ✗
That matches choice C — C is correct.
